articles.title: Mann Ki Baat Episode 134: From Heatwave Alerts, Water Conservation To Record-Breaking Athletes, Key Takeaways From PM Modi’s Address - The Sunday Guardian
articles.full_text:
In the 134th episode of Mann Ki Baat, PM Modi urged citizens to take precautions against heatwaves, conserve water, support community service, and care for birds and animals. He also praised India's record-breaking athletes, highlighted cultural heritage, astronomy, youth achievements and local traditions.
Mann Ki Baat Episode 134: On Sunday, Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke to the nation in 134th episode of his monthly radio programme Mann Ki Baat, covering a range of topics from heatwave vigilance and water conservation to India's sporting legacy, cultural heritage and the role of youth in the country. Prime Minister spoke about successful examples from across the country, and called upon citizens to participate in nation-building efforts, both online and offline. Here are the key things he discussed.
With many parts of the country experiencing high temperatures, the Prime Minister urged people to take measures to protect themselves from heat-related illnesses. He told citizens to remain hydrated, limit time spent in the sun and heed directives from government agencies.
"It is very hot in most parts of the country right now. Strong sun, hot winds, it is very important to take care of yourself in such weather. Keep drinking water. If at all you have to go out in the sun, do so with caution. Don't forget the guidelines issued by various government departments in this regard," he said.
Modi also urged people to keep themselves properly hydrated with dietary staples like lassi, chaas and aam panna, to stay healthy during summer months. He appealed to people to take care of children, the elderly and other vulnerable groups during extreme weather. The Prime Minister also urged people to keep water available for birds and other animals affected by the heat.
"Sattu sherbet in Bihar, Jharkhand, and Eastern Uttar Pradesh is simply amazing - it fills the stomach and provides strength. Kokum sherbet and Sol Kadhi in Konkan and Goa. Panakam, Neer Mor, Sambaram in South India, and Bael Pana in Odisha aren't just drinks, they're a part of the traditions of different regions of India. And they also reflect the spirit of 'Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat.'," Modi said, news agency ANI reports
Emphasising the need to conserve water in summer, the Prime Minister praised efforts undertaken by people to protect dwindling water resources. He emphasized that water conservation was not the sole responsibility of the government, but a shared responsibility of people.
He called upon citizens to continue initiating campaigns to conserve water at the grassroots level, and cited efforts underway to protect water bodies across the country.
A major theme of the address was India's recent success in athletics. Modi congratulated Gurindervir Singh, Vishal TK, Tejaswin Shankar, Dev Meena and Kuldeep Kumar for breaking new national records at the National Senior Athletics Federation Competition in Ranchi.
He drew particular attention to the intense duelling between Gurindervir Singh and Animesh Kujur, whose rivalry ensured that the national record for men’s 100m sprint was broken three times in two days' time. He said the achievement defines the strength of Indian athletics and urged youngsters to take up sports.
The Prime Minister also discussed the rising popularity of astronomy amongst young Indians and urged students to join astronomy clubs and visit planetariums as it stimulates curiosity, scientific thinking and a spirit of exploration.
"We Indians have always had a special fascination with astronomy. Centuries-old observatories still exist in our country, where amazing mathematical discoveries have been made. Whether it's navigation, the almanack, or our festivals, they're all connected to the sky and the stars. Astronomy has aroused curiosity in every generation, inspiring exploration, and today's youth also show a great deal of enthusiasm for it. You may have noticed that astronomy clubs are rapidly gaining popularity across the country," Modi said.
Mr Modi emphasized how technology is helping promote India's vast cultural heritage. He noted the digitisation of significant historical documents and records underway aimed at making them more widely accessible to the public. Mr Modi said technology is helping connect younger people with India's past while ensuring that valuable archival material is preserved for future generations.
Responding to the issue of India’s historic heritage, Mr Modi said the return of original Chola-era copper plates from the Netherlands will go a long way towards shedding light on the achievements of the Chola Empire, its maritime potency and global connections.
The Prime Minister also said the discovery of rare copper plates which were found in Chhattisgarh nearly 1400 to 1500 years back under the Gyan Bharatam Abhiyan constitute an important landmark in learning about India's glorious past.
Modi complimented the talented students, innovators and young achievers from various parts of the country for their achievements in academics, sports and community development. He said the achievements of India's youth reflect the country's potential and act as a source of inspiration for generations to come.
In a light hearted part of the programme, Modi spoke about the fantastic fruit and said its love affair with summers in the country is a deep-rooted phenomenon. He said people have an emotional connection and love for mangoes and their multiple varieties across the country and it is a fruit to cherish.
The Prime Minister also talked about India's rich cultural diversity and heritage with indigenous products. He specifically spoke about Kalari cheese from Jammu and Kashmir, which is getting an increasing amount of recognition. He urged citizens to support local products, local artisans and traditions as part of the wider effort to promote Indian culture.
To bring the episode to a close, Modi spoke about food for thought, such as health, environmental responsibility, sports and youth empowerment as well as celebrating our heritage and community bonding. He urged citizens to shoulder parts of societal responsibility by helping each other and contributing to the nation, while staying safe during this summer season.
